political.s: <cite index="2-12">Morgan Stanley PAC contributes on a bipartisan basis to U.S. federal candidates and political committees.</cite> <cite index="6-1">Morgan Stanley raised $839,685 in the 2023-2024 election cycle.</cite> The provided FEC data shows 31% Republican and 51% Democrat giving (totaling $475K), confirming a bipartisan but left-leaning donation pattern aligned with stated policy.

charity.s: <cite index="13-15,13-16">Morgan Stanley Foundation and International Foundation focus on giving children a healthy start, encouraging employee volunteer efforts, and creating college and career pathways for first generation and low-income students.</cite> <cite index="13-17">The firm funds programs focusing on mental health, physical health, food insecurity and play for children.</cite>
charity.focus: Children's health, education, mental health, food insecurity

environment.s: <cite index="28-5">In 2020, Morgan Stanley was the first major US-headquartered global financial services firm to commit to achieving net zero in financed emissions by 2050.</cite> <cite index="28-6">In 2022, the company achieved carbon neutrality across direct Scope 1 emissions, indirect Scope 2 from energy purchasing, and Scope 3 from business travel and owned assets.</cite> <cite index="28-9">Through 2022, Morgan Stanley mobilised US$700 billion in capital, including US$550 billion in low-carbon and green solutions.</cite>

labor.s: <cite index="32-9,32-10">Former Morgan Stanley advisors have pursued claims in arbitration alleging the firm improperly withheld deferred compensation after departure, arguing Morgan Stanley violates ERISA.</cite> <cite index="34-3">Core values include Commit to Diversity and Inclusion and Give Back.</cite> Deferred compensation disputes suggest labor practice concerns for employees.

dei.s: <cite index="42-2,42-3">Morgan Stanley appears to be among latest banks to de-emphasize mentions of diversity, equity and inclusion in SEC filings, with this year's annual filing not featuring the "Diversity and Inclusion" subheading.</cite> <cite index="41-1,41-2">Morgan Stanley has DEI rating of 4.0 out of 5 stars on Glassdoor, equal to average for Financial Services sector.</cite> The firm maintains employee resource networks but has reduced explicit DEI emphasis.

animals.s: Not applicable for financial services firm.

guns.s: Not applicable for financial services firm.

privacy.s: As a financial services firm, Morgan Stanley handles significant client data. Limited public disclosures of specific privacy practices are available in search results. Company policies address confidential information protection but comprehensive privacy grading cannot be fully assessed.

execPay.s: <cite index="52-1">CEO Ted Pick earned $15.4M in salary, stock awards, and bonus in 2026 — a 128:1 ratio to median worker pay.</cite> <cite index="54-1">Morgan Stanley paid CEO Ted Pick $45 million for 2025.</cite> The steep executive-to-worker pay disparity and significant year-over-year increases reflect substantial CEO compensation relative to workforce pay.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
